violations.text: The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Staff to call for service. 3-bay sink to be used for sanitizing all equipment. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2025

Per interview of staff, knives are only washed, rinsed, and sanitized at the end of the day. The surfaces of equipment that contacts time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as eggs, meats and cheeses must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized at least every four hours. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Staff labeled during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

Working containers of syrups are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Staff labeled during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Staff removed from water, dry container was obtained. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

The auto-dispenser for quaternary ammonium sanitizer at the 3-bay sink was dispensing at a concentration of less than 200 ppm. Staff to call for service. 3-bay sink to be manually set-up until serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan guards in the walk-in cooler have an accumulation of white residue. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024
